message:
feat(rchd): add Unix SIGHUP signal handling for config reload

Platform-specific improvements to the daemon main loop:
- Unix: tokio::select! with SIGHUP signal handling for reload trigger
- Cleaner shutdown channel integration
- Improved connection error logging

This enables 'kill -HUP <pid>' as an alternative to 'rch daemon reload'
for triggering configuration updates on Unix systems.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -97,7 +97,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2juk","title":"Doctor --fix Auto-Starts Daemon","description":"# Doctor --fix Auto-Starts Daemon

## Problem Statement
When running `rch doctor`, the daemon status is checked and reported:
```
Daemon
  ⚠ Daemon is not running
    Socket not found: /run/user/1000/rch.sock
    Suggestion: Start with: rch daemon start
```

But `rch doctor --fix` doesn't actually start the daemon. Users have to manually run `rch daemon start` separately.

## Solution: Doctor --fix Starts Daemon if Not Running
When --fix is passed and daemon is not running, start it automatically.

## Implementation Plan

### 1. Add daemon start logic to check_daemon function
In `rch/src/doctor.rs`, find the daemon check section and add fix logic:

```rust
async fn check_daemon(
    checks: &mut Vec<CheckResult>,
    ctx: &OutputContext,
    options: &DoctorOptions,
) {
    let style = ctx.theme();
    let socket_path = Path::new(crate::daemon::DEFAULT_SOCKET_PATH);

    if !ctx.is_json() {
        println!("{}", style.highlight("Daemon"));
        println!();
    }

    // Track fix info
    let mut fix_applied = false;
    let mut fix_message = String::new();

    let mut result = if socket_path.exists() {
        // Existing check logic...
        CheckResult {
            category: "daemon".to_string(),
            name: "daemon_running".to_string(),
            status: CheckStatus::Pass,
            message: "Daemon is running".to_string(),
            details: Some(socket_path.display().to_string()),
            suggestion: None,
            fixable: false,
            fix_applied: false,
            fix_message: None,
        }
    } else {
        CheckResult {
            category: "daemon".to_string(),
            name: "daemon_running".to_string(),
            status: CheckStatus::Warning,
            message: "Daemon is not running".to_string(),
            details: Some(format!("Socket not found: {}", socket_path.display())),
            suggestion: Some("Start with: rch daemon start".to_string()),
            fixable: true,  // Mark as fixable!
            fix_applied: false,
            fix_message: None,
        }
    };

    // Try to fix if requested
    if options.fix && result.fixable && result.status != CheckStatus::Pass {
        if options.dry_run {
            fix_message = "Would start RCH daemon".to_string();
            if !ctx.is_json() {
                println!(
                    "  {} {}",
                    StatusIndicator::DryRun.display(style),
                    &fix_message
                );
            }
            result.fix_message = Some(fix_message);
        } else {
            match start_daemon_for_doctor().await {
                Ok(()) => {
                    fix_applied = true;
                    fix_message = "Started RCH daemon".to_string();
                    if !ctx.is_json() {
                        println!(
                            "  {} {}",
                            StatusIndicator::Fixed.display(style),
                            &fix_message
                        );
                    }
                    result.status = CheckStatus::Pass;
                    result.message = "Daemon started (fixed)".to_string();
                    result.details = Some(socket_path.display().to_string());
                    result.fixable = false;
                    result.fix_applied = true;
                    result.fix_message = Some(fix_message);
                }
                Err(e) => {
                    fix_message = format!("Failed to start daemon: {}", e);
                    if !ctx.is_json() {
                        println!(
                            "  {} {}",
                            StatusIndicator::Error.display(style),
                            &fix_message
                        );
                    }
                    result.fix_message = Some(fix_message);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    print_check_result(&result, ctx);
    checks.push(result);
}
```

### 2. Create daemon start helper function
```rust
async fn start_daemon_for_doctor() -> Result<()> {
    use tokio::process::Command;
    use std::process::Stdio;
    
    let rchd_path = which_rchd()
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow::anyhow!("rchd not found in PATH"))?;
    
    debug!("Starting daemon: {}", rchd_path.display());
    
    let mut cmd = Command::new("nohup");
    cmd.arg(&rchd_path)
        .stdout(Stdio::null())
        .stderr(Stdio::null())
        .stdin(Stdio::null())
        .kill_on_drop(false);
    
    cmd.spawn()?;
    
    // Wait for socket to appear
    let socket_path = Path::new(crate::daemon::DEFAULT_SOCKET_PATH);
    for i in 0..30 {
        tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
        if socket_path.exists() {
            debug!("Socket appeared after {}ms", (i + 1) * 100);
            return Ok(());
        }
    }
    
    Err(anyhow::anyhow!("Daemon started but socket not found after 3s"))
}
```

### 3. Ensure fix order: hooks before daemon
In the main doctor flow, ensure hooks are fixed before daemon is started. This matters because:
- Daemon auto-installs hooks on startup (bd-1emo)
- If hooks are already installed by doctor --fix (bd-3pam), daemon sees them as already installed
- This ensures idempotency

The execution order in doctor should be:
1. System checks
2. SSH checks (with --fix for permissions)
3. **Hook checks (with --fix to install)** ← First
4. **Daemon checks (with --fix to start)** ← Second
5. Worker checks (only if daemon is running)

### 4. JSON Output Format
When running `rch doctor --fix --json`:

```json
{
  "checks": [
    {
      "category": "daemon",
      "name": "daemon_running",
      "status": "pass",
      "message": "Daemon started (fixed)",
      "details": "/run/user/1000/rch.sock",
      "suggestion": null,
      "fixable": false,
      "fix_applied": true,
      "fix_message": "Started RCH daemon"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total": 10,
    "passed": 10,
    "warnings": 0,
    "failed": 0,
    "fixed": 2
  }
}
```

When running `rch doctor --fix --dry-run --json`:
```json
{
  "checks": [
    {
      "category": "daemon",
      "name": "daemon_running",
      "status": "warning",
      "message": "Daemon is not running",
      "details": "Socket not found: /run/user/1000/rch.sock",
      "suggestion": "Start with: rch daemon start",
      "fixable": true,
      "fix_applied": false,
      "fix_message": "Would start RCH daemon"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total": 10,
    "passed": 8,
    "warnings": 2,
    "failed": 0,
    "fixed": 0,
    "would_fix": 2
  }
}
```

### Code Locations
- `rch/src/doctor.rs` - check_daemon function (around line 920)
- `rch/src/commands.rs:3440-3460` - Reference daemon start logic
- `rch/src/doctor.rs:771` - Pattern for SSH permission fix
- `rch/src/doctor.rs` - which_rchd() function

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `rch doctor --fix` starts daemon if not running
- [ ] `rch doctor --fix --dry-run` shows what would be done
- [ ] Socket appearance is verified after starting (3s timeout)
- [ ] Start failure is reported clearly with error message
- [ ] Works correctly with bd-3pam (hooks fixed first in execution order)
- [ ] Daemon marked as fixable=true when not running
- [ ] JSON output includes fix_applied and fix_message fields
- [ ] Summary counts fixed correctly
- [ ] No-op if daemon already running
- [ ] rchd binary not found is handled gracefully

### Logging Requirements
Terminal output (non-JSON):
```
Daemon
  ✓ Fixed: Started RCH daemon
  ✓ Daemon started (fixed)
    /run/user/1000/rch.sock
```

Or on dry-run:
```
Daemon
  ○ Would fix: Would start RCH daemon
  ⚠ Daemon is not running
    Socket not found: /run/user/1000/rch.sock
    Suggestion: Start with: rch daemon start
```

Or on failure:
```
Daemon
  ✗ Failed to start daemon: rchd not found in PATH
  ⚠ Daemon is not running
    Socket not found: /run/user/1000/rch.sock
    Suggestion: Start with: rch daemon start
```

### Testing Notes
- Test --fix starts daemon when not running
- Test --fix is no-op when daemon running
- Test --fix handles missing rchd binary gracefully
- Test --fix handles spawn failures
- Test --fix handles socket timeout (daemon started but no socket)
- Test --fix --dry-run doesn't start daemon
- Test JSON output format with fix_applied=true
- Test JSON output format with fix_applied=false (dry-run)
- Test order: hooks fixed before daemon started
- Test summary counts fixed and would_fix correctly

### Dependencies
- Depends on bd-3pam (Doctor --fix Actually Fixes Hooks) for consistent fix pattern","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-26T04:55:29.978458791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T05:20:42.370795433Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2juk","depends_on_id":"bd-3pam","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-26T04:57:28.215203393Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2kr0","title":"Test: cargo build Remote Execution","description":"## Purpose
Test that `cargo build` commands are correctly offloaded to real workers and produce valid binaries locally.

## MANDATORY Logging
This test MUST use TestLogger (from bd-1tq2) for all phases:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_basic","phase":"setup","msg":"Preparing build test","data":{"fixture":"hello_world","worker":"css"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_basic","phase":"execute_local","msg":"Local build baseline","data":{"cmd":"cargo build","exit_code":0,"duration_ms":5432}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_basic","phase":"execute_remote","msg":"Remote build","data":{"cmd":"cargo build","exit_code":0,"worker":"css","duration_ms":4210}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build_basic","phase":"verify","msg":"Binary verification","data":{"path":"target/debug/hello_world","exists":true,"runs":true}}
```

## Test Cases
1. Basic cargo build:
   - Command: `cargo build`
   - Expected: target/debug/hello_world binary exists locally
   - Verify: binary runs and produces expected output
   - Log: local baseline, remote execution, verification
   
2. Release build:
   - Command: `cargo build --release`
   - Expected: target/release/hello_world binary exists
   - Verify: binary is optimized (smaller, no debug symbols)
   - Log: size comparison debug vs release

3. Specific target:
   - Command: `cargo build --bin hello_world`
   - Expected: only specified binary built
   - Log: target specification

4. Workspace build:
   - Command: `cargo build --workspace`
   - Expected: all workspace members built
   - Log: packages built

5. Package-specific:
   - Command: `cargo build -p specific_package`
   - Expected: only that package built
   - Log: package filter

## Verification
- Binary exists and is executable (log path, exists, permissions)
- Binary produces correct output when run (log execution result)
- Build artifacts in target/ match expected structure (log artifact list)
- No leftover artifacts on worker (log cleanup check)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All test cases pass on real worker
- [ ] Binaries byte-for-byte correct (hash match optional)
- [ ] Exit codes propagated correctly (0 for success)
- [ ] ALL phases logged with structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:23:12.531996653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:28:35.512321320Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:28:35.511895527Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-12hi (Cargo Compilation E2E); specific cargo build case already covered.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2kr0","depends_on_id":"bd-12hi","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:23:25.035675866Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2kr0","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:23:26.036582861Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ld5","title":"Add rich output to rch compile command feedback","description":"Enhance rch compile (the primary command) with rich progress feedback:
- Initial banner showing job submission with job ID
- Transfer progress bar for workspace sync (files synced, bytes transferred)
- Compilation status with elapsed time counter
- Artifact retrieval progress bar
- Final summary panel with timing breakdown

Technical requirements:
- All output to stderr to not interfere with compiler stdout/stderr passthrough
- Detect if running in hook context and suppress rich output entirely
- Use inline progress bars that update in place (carriage return technique)
- Show transfer speeds in human-readable format (MB/s)
- Include cache status indicator (HIT/MISS with appropriate coloring)
- Graceful handling of terminal resize during progress display

Flow visualization:

[rch] ↓ Retrieving artifacts... 156 files ████████████ 100%

│ Total: 52.1s (sync: 3.2s, build: 45.2s,     │
│        artifacts: 3.7s)                      │
│ Cache: MISS │ Worker: worker1               │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────╯","notes":"Implemented rich compile feedback: job banner, transfer progress (sync/artifacts), compile progress for build/check/clippy/doc/bench, and final summary panel (timing + cache HIT/MISS). Added streaming rsync helpers and transfer stats.  {"id":"bd-2m7j","title":"Idea: Enforce min_local_time_ms + speedup gating","description":"## Background
RCH advertises min_local_time_ms and remote_speedup_threshold but the hook currently does not gate offloading with these signals. This risks offloading tiny builds where overhead dominates, creating a mismatch between documented behavior and real behavior.

## Goals
- Honor min_local_time_ms and remote_speedup_threshold at hook decision time.
- Use real timing history + worker speed scores to estimate local time and speedup.
- Preserve fail-open behavior and sub-5ms classification budget.

## Non-Goals
- Changing classification rules or supported commands.
- Introducing new telemetry backends.

## Design / Approach
1) Extend build history data model to store remote duration + estimated local for each intercepted command (per project + kind).
2) Hook computes: predicted_local_ms (from history + worker speed score) and predicted_speedup.
3) Skip remote if predicted_local_ms < min_local_time_ms OR predicted_speedup < remote_speedup_threshold.
4) Surface the decision in verbose output + JSON diagnostics.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add timing fields to command result pipeline (hook -> daemon selection -> hook).
- Record per-command timing metrics in a lightweight local history store (ring buffer).
- Add estimation logic: fallback order = history median -> worker speed score -> conservative default.
- Integrate gating in hook after classification and before worker selection.
- Update docs/config help to align behavior.

## Tests
- Unit: estimation math (min_local_time_ms + speedup threshold edge cases).
- Unit: history fallback order.
- Integration: hook skips remote when predicted local < threshold.
- Integration: hook still offloads when predicted speedup >= threshold.
- E2E: small project compile uses local, large project offloads (mock timings).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Hook honors both thresholds with deterministic behavior.
- Verbose output clearly states gating reason.
- No regression in non-compilation latency budget.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-2m7j.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- No history yet: allow remote but log "insufficient data" to avoid false local skips.
- History store unavailable/corrupt: fail-open without blocking hook.
- Threshold validation: clamp invalid values and surface in lint/logs.

## E2E Outline
- Small project + high min_local_time_ms -> local allowed with clear reason.
- Inject mock history with high speedup -> remote allowed; verify reason string.
- Verify gating does not exceed classification latency budget (<5ms).

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/hook.rs: gating decision uses min_local_time_ms and speedup threshold.
- rch-common/src/types.rs: any new timing structs serialize with defaults.
- rch/src/transfer.rs: estimation fallback order (history -> speedscore -> default).

## E2E Script Notes
- scripts/e2e_bd-2m7j.sh: inject mock history + speedscore, verify skip vs offload.
- Assert verbose output includes gating reason and estimated local time.

@@ -113,7 +113,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ufg","title":"Idea: Rsync bandwidth limit control","description":"## Background
Large syncs can saturate network links and disrupt other work. A configurable bandwidth limit helps in shared environments.

## Goals
- Add config option for rsync `--bwlimit`.
- Support per-project override.

## Design / Approach
- Extend transfer config with `bwlimit_kbps` (0 = unlimited).
- Add `--bwlimit` to rsync upload and artifact retrieval commands.
- Document defaults and usage.

## Tasks / Subtasks
- Add config parsing + validation.
- Wire into rsync command builder.
- Update docs + examples.

## Tests
- Unit: config parsing.
- Integration: rsync args include bwlimit when set.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Bandwidth limit can be configured and takes effect.
- Default remains unlimited.

## Logging & E2E
- E2E script: scripts/e2e_bd-2ufg.sh (one script per bead unless intentionally combined).
- Logging format: JSONL via TestLogger (tests/true_e2e/logging.rs) or scripts/test_lib.sh log_json.
- Required fields: ts, test, phase, worker, command, bytes_transferred, duration_ms, result, error (if any).
- Phases to log (as applicable): classify, select, sync_up, exec, sync_down, cleanup, summary.
- Include a final summary block with pass/fail counts and total elapsed time.

## Edge Cases & UX
- bwlimit=0/unset -> no flag.
- Apply to both upload and artifact retrieval.
- Invalid values are rejected with clear errors.

## E2E Outline
- Set bwlimit -> rsync args include --bwlimit.
- Unset -> no --bwlimit present.

## Unit Tests (Detailed)
- rch/src/transfer.rs: rsync args include --bwlimit when set.

 {"id":"bd-2val","title":"Create Infrastructure Smoke Test (test_infrastructure_smoke)","description":"## Purpose
Create a quick smoke test that validates the entire e2e test infrastructure works before running the full test suite.

## Why This Matters
- Full e2e suite takes time; quick validation saves debugging time
- Infrastructure issues should be caught immediately
- Serves as documentation of "happy path" behavior
- CI can fail fast on infrastructure problems

## Smoke Test: test_infrastructure_smoke

### What It Tests (in order)
1. **Logging Setup**:
   - Create TestLogger
   - Write log entry
   - Verify file exists and is valid JSON

2. **Worker Discovery**:
   - Load workers_test.toml
   - Find at least one worker
   - (Or skip gracefully if none configured)

3. **Daemon Communication**:
   - Connect to daemon socket
   - Send health check request
   - Receive valid response

4. **SSH Connectivity**:
   - SSH to first available worker
   - Run `echo 'smoke test'`
   - Verify output

5. **Project Sync**:
   - Sync hello_world fixture to worker
   - Verify files arrived (`ls -la` on worker)

6. **Remote Execution**:
   - Run `cargo build` on synced project
   - Verify exit code 0

7. **Artifact Retrieval**:
   - Retrieve built binary
   - Verify binary exists locally
   - Run binary, verify output

8. **Output Comparison**:
   - Compare remote output to expected
   - Verify normalization works

9. **Cleanup**:
   - Remove worker project directory
   - Release any resources

### Implementation

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/smoke.rs

#[test]
fn test_infrastructure_smoke() {
    // This test has no dependencies on other e2e tests
    // It validates the infrastructure works end-to-end
    
    let logger = TestLogger::new("smoke_test");
    logger.info("Starting smoke test", &[]);
    
    // Step 1: Logging
    assert!(logger.log_path().exists(), "Log file not created");
    
    // Step 2: Worker discovery
    let workers = discover_workers();
    if workers.is_empty() {
        logger.info("SKIP: No workers configured", &[]);
        return; // Graceful skip
    }
    
    // Step 3: Daemon
    let daemon = connect_to_daemon().expect("Daemon not running");
    assert!(daemon.health_check().is_ok(), "Daemon unhealthy");
    
    // Step 4: SSH
    let worker = &workers[0];
    let ssh_result = worker.run_command("echo 'smoke test'");
    assert_eq!(ssh_result.stdout.trim(), "smoke test");
    
    // Step 5: Sync
    let fixture_path = fixtures_dir().join("hello_world");
    let sync_result = sync_to_worker(worker, &fixture_path);
    assert!(sync_result.is_ok(), "Sync failed: {:?}", sync_result);
    
    // Step 6: Remote execution
    let build_result = worker.run_command("cargo build");
    assert_eq!(build_result.exit_code, 0, "Build failed: {}", build_result.stderr);
    
    // Step 7: Artifact retrieval
    let artifacts = retrieve_artifacts(worker);
    let binary_path = artifacts.join("target/debug/hello_world");
    assert!(binary_path.exists(), "Binary not retrieved");
    
    // Step 8: Run binary
    let run_result = std::process::Command::new(&binary_path).output().unwrap();
    assert!(run_result.status.success());
    assert!(String::from_utf8_lossy(&run_result.stdout).contains("Hello"));
    
    // Step 9: Cleanup
    cleanup_worker_project(worker);
    
    logger.info("Smoke test PASSED", &[("total_ms", &logger.elapsed_ms().to_string())]);
}
```

### Run Independently
```bash
# Run just the smoke test
cargo test --features true-e2e test_infrastructure_smoke -- --nocapture
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Smoke test covers all infrastructure components
- [ ] Clear error messages for each failure mode
- [ ] Skips gracefully when no workers
- [ ] Runs in < 30 seconds (not a full build)
- [ ] Logs every step for debugging
- [ ] Can be run independently of other tests","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:49.194770379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:37:07.576220526Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T00:37:07.570503246Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2val","depends_on_id":"bd-17tg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:34:04.539136800Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2val","depends_on_id":"bd-1tq2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:34:01.343866240Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2val","depends_on_id":"bd-2a7t","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:34:07.651699006Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2val","depends_on_id":"bd-3r2b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:34:03.044357438Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2val","depends_on_id":"bd-3saj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:33:59.670672749Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2val","depends_on_id":"bd-8l6b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:34:05.813587185Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2y2a","title":"Test Logging Infrastructure for Rich UI Tests","description":"## Comprehensive Test Logging Infrastructure

Create detailed logging infrastructure for all rich_rust integration tests to enable debugging when tests fail.

### Why This Matters

1. Rich output tests involve terminal state, escape codes, timing - hard to debug
2. CI environments may behave differently than local development
3. Need to capture stdout, stderr, timing, and terminal state for diagnosis
4. Should produce artifacts that can be attached to GitHub issues

### Implementation

```rust
// rch-common/src/testing/log.rs

use std::fs::{self, File};
use std::io::Write;
use std::path::PathBuf;
use std::time::{Duration, Instant, SystemTime};
use serde::{Serialize, Deserialize};

/// Test execution log entry
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TestLogEntry {
    pub timestamp: String,
    pub test_name: String,
    pub phase: String, // "setup", "execute", "verify", "teardown"
    pub message: String,
    pub data: Option<serde_json::Value>,
    pub duration_ms: Option<u64>,
}

/// Test result with detailed diagnostics
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TestResult {
    pub test_name: String,
    pub passed: bool,
    pub duration_ms: u64,
    pub stdout: String,
    pub stderr: String,
    pub exit_code: Option<i32>,
    pub environment: std::collections::HashMap<String, String>,
    pub terminal_info: TerminalInfo,
    pub log_entries: Vec<TestLogEntry>,
    pub error_message: Option<String>,
}

#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TerminalInfo {
    pub is_tty: bool,
    pub width: Option<u16>,
    pub height: Option<u16>,
    pub term_var: Option<String>,
    pub colorterm_var: Option<String>,
    pub no_color_set: bool,
    pub force_color_set: bool,
}

impl TerminalInfo {
    pub fn capture() -> Self {
        Self {
            is_tty: atty::is(atty::Stream::Stderr),
            width: terminal_size::terminal_size().map(|(w, _)| w.0),
            height: terminal_size::terminal_size().map(|(_, h)| h.0),
            term_var: std::env::var("TERM").ok(),
            colorterm_var: std::env::var("COLORTERM").ok(),
            no_color_set: std::env::var("NO_COLOR").is_ok(),
            force_color_set: std::env::var("FORCE_COLOR").is_ok(),
        }
    }
}

/// Test logger that writes structured JSON logs
pub struct TestLogger {
    test_name: String,
    log_file: PathBuf,
    entries: Vec<TestLogEntry>,
    start_time: Instant,
    file: File,
}

impl TestLogger {
    pub fn new(test_name: &str) -> std::io::Result<Self> {
        let log_dir = PathBuf::from("target/test-logs");
        fs::create_dir_all(&log_dir)?;
        
        let timestamp = chrono::Utc::now().format("%Y%m%d_%H%M%S");
        let log_file = log_dir.join(format!("{}_{}.jsonl", test_name, timestamp));
        let file = File::create(&log_file)?;
        
        Ok(Self {
            test_name: test_name.to_string(),
            log_file,
            entries: Vec::new(),
            start_time: Instant::now(),
            file,
        })
    }
    
    pub fn log(&mut self, phase: &str, message: &str, data: Option<serde_json::Value>) {
        let entry = TestLogEntry {
            timestamp: chrono::Utc::now().to_rfc3339(),
            test_name: self.test_name.clone(),
            phase: phase.to_string(),
            message: message.to_string(),
            data,
            duration_ms: Some(self.start_time.elapsed().as_millis() as u64),
        };
        
        if let Ok(json) = serde_json::to_string(&entry) {
            let _ = writeln!(self.file, "{}", json);
        }
        
        self.entries.push(entry);
    }
    
    pub fn log_stdout(&mut self, content: &str) {
        self.log("stdout", "Captured stdout", Some(serde_json::json!({
            "content": content,
            "bytes": content.len(),
            "has_ansi": content.contains("\x1b["),
        })));
    }
    
    pub fn log_stderr(&mut self, content: &str) {
        self.log("stderr", "Captured stderr", Some(serde_json::json!({
            "content": content,
            "bytes": content.len(),
            "has_ansi": content.contains("\x1b["),
        })));
    }
    
    pub fn log_timing(&mut self, operation: &str, duration: Duration) {
        self.log("timing", &format!("{} took {:?}", operation, duration), 
            Some(serde_json::json!({
                "operation": operation,
                "duration_ms": duration.as_millis(),
            })));
    }
    
    pub fn finalize(self, passed: bool, error: Option<&str>) -> TestResult {
        TestResult {
            test_name: self.test_name,
            passed,
            duration_ms: self.start_time.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,
            stdout: String::new(), // Set by caller
            stderr: String::new(), // Set by caller
            exit_code: None,
            environment: std::env::vars().collect(),
            terminal_info: TerminalInfo::capture(),
            log_entries: self.entries,
            error_message: error.map(String::from),
        }
    }
}

/// Macro for test logging
#[macro_export]
macro_rules! test_log {
    ($logger:expr, $phase:expr, $msg:expr) => {
        $logger.log($phase, $msg, None);
    };
    ($logger:expr, $phase:expr, $msg:expr, $data:expr) => {
        $logger.log($phase, $msg, Some(serde_json::json!($data)));
    };
}
```

### Bash Test Logging Helper

```bash
# scripts/test_lib.sh - Source this in test scripts

LOG_FILE=""
TEST_START=""

init_test_log() {
    local test_name="$1"
    local log_dir="target/test-logs"
    mkdir -p "$log_dir"
    LOG_FILE="$log_dir/${test_name}_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).jsonl"
    TEST_START=$(date +%s%N)
    echo "Test log: $LOG_FILE"
}

log_json() {
    local phase="$1"
    local message="$2"
    local data="${3:-null}"
    local elapsed=$((($(date +%s%N) - TEST_START) / 1000000))
    
    jq -n \
        --arg ts "$(date -Iseconds)" \
        --arg phase "$phase" \
        --arg msg "$message" \
        --argjson data "$data" \
        --argjson ms "$elapsed" \
        '{timestamp:$ts, phase:$phase, message:$msg, data:$data, duration_ms:$ms}' \
        >> "$LOG_FILE"
}

log_stdout_capture() {
    local file="$1"
    local content="$(cat "$file" | head -c 10000)"  # Limit size
    local bytes="$(wc -c < "$file")"
    local has_ansi="false"
    grep -qP '\x1b\[' "$file" && has_ansi="true"
    
    log_json "stdout" "Captured stdout" "{\"bytes\":$bytes,\"has_ansi\":$has_ansi}"
}

log_terminal_info() {
    log_json "setup" "Terminal info" "{
        \"is_tty\":$([ -t 2 ] && echo true || echo false),
        \"term\":\"${TERM:-unknown}\",
        \"no_color\":$([ -n "${NO_COLOR:-}" ] && echo true || echo false),
        \"force_color\":$([ -n "${FORCE_COLOR:-}" ] && echo true || echo false)
    }"
}
```

### Usage in Tests

```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
source scripts/test_lib.sh

init_test_log "hook_non_interference"
log_terminal_info

log_json "setup" "Building rch" '{"features":"rich-ui"}'
cargo build --features rich-ui --release

log_json "execute" "Running hook test" null
# ... run test ...

log_stdout_capture /tmp/stdout.txt
log_json "verify" "Checking JSON validity" '{"result":"pass"}'
```

 {"id":"bd-2zc4","title":"Implement Worker Availability Check & Skip Mechanism","description":"## Purpose
Create a mechanism to automatically skip e2e tests when no real workers are available, avoiding CI failures in environments without worker access.

## MANDATORY Logging
All skip decisions MUST be logged using TestLogger:

```json
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build","phase":"skip_check","msg":"Checking worker availability","data":{"config_path":"/path/to/workers_test.toml"}}
{"ts":"...","level":"INFO","test":"test_cargo_build","phase":"skip_check","msg":"Workers unavailable, skipping test","data":{"reason":"no_workers_configured","skip":true}}
```

## Requirements
1. Check worker availability before running tests
2. Skip tests gracefully with clear message when no workers
3. Work with both `#[ignore]` and runtime skipping
4. Support CI mode vs local development mode
5. Log all skip decisions with structured JSON

## Implementation
```rust
use crate::true_e2e::logging::TestLogger;

/// Check if real workers are available for e2e tests
pub fn workers_available(logger: &mut TestLogger) -> bool {
    logger.phase("worker_check");
    
    // Check RCH_E2E_SKIP if set
    if env::var("RCH_E2E_SKIP").is_ok() {
        logger.info("E2E tests disabled via env", &[("env", "RCH_E2E_SKIP")]);
        return false;
    }
    
    // Try to load test worker config
    let config_path = env::var("RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG")
        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "tests/e2e/workers_test.toml".to_string());
    logger.debug("Loading worker config", &[("path", &config_path)]);
    
    match load_worker_config(&config_path) {
        Ok(config) => {
            let available = ping_workers(&config, logger);
            logger.info("Worker check complete", &[
                ("configured", &config.workers.len().to_string()),
                ("reachable", &available.to_string()),
            ]);
            available > 0
        }
        Err(e) => {
            logger.warn("Config load failed", &[("error", &e.to_string())]);
            false
        }
    }
}

/// Macro for tests that need real workers
macro_rules! require_workers {
    ($logger:expr) => {
        if !workers_available($logger) {
            $logger.info("SKIP: No real workers available", &[]);
            return;
        }
    };
}
```

## Environment Variables
- `RCH_E2E_SKIP=1` - Force skip all e2e tests
- `RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG=/path/to/config` - Override worker config
- `RCH_E2E_TIMEOUT=60` - Override test timeout

## Unit Tests

```rust
// tests/true_e2e/tests/skip_tests.rs

#[test]
fn test_skip_when_env_set() {
    env::set_var("RCH_E2E_SKIP", "1");
    let mut logger = TestLogger::new("test_skip_env").unwrap();
    assert!(!workers_available(&mut logger));
    env::remove_var("RCH_E2E_SKIP");
}

#[test]
fn test_skip_when_no_config() {
    env::set_var("RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG", "/nonexistent/path.toml");
    let mut logger = TestLogger::new("test_skip_config").unwrap();
    assert!(!workers_available(&mut logger));
    env::remove_var("RCH_E2E_WORKERS_CONFIG");
}

#[test]
fn test_skip_logs_reason() {
    let mut logger = TestLogger::new("test_skip_logs").unwrap();
    let _ = workers_available(&mut logger);
    let log_content = std::fs::read_to_string(logger.log_path()).unwrap();
    assert!(log_content.contains("worker_check"));
}

#[test]
fn test_require_workers_macro_returns_early() {
    // Test that the macro causes early return when no workers
    let mut logger = TestLogger::new("test_macro").unwrap();
    env::set_var("RCH_E2E_SKIP", "1");
    
    let executed = std::cell::Cell::new(false);
    (|| {
        require_workers!(&mut logger);
        executed.set(true);
    })();
    
    assert!(!executed.get(), "Should have returned early");
    env::remove_var("RCH_E2E_SKIP");
}

#[test]
fn test_worker_ping_timeout() {
    // Verify ping times out quickly for unreachable workers
    let start = Instant::now();
    let mut logger = TestLogger::new("test_timeout").unwrap();
    // ... test with invalid host
    assert!(start.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(10));
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests skip gracefully when workers unavailable
- [ ] CI does not fail due to missing workers
- [ ] Clear skip messages in test output
- [ ] Local development can run tests when workers available
- [ ] ALL skip decisions logged as structured JSON
